meaning: adj.善于嚣叫的,脾气坏的,爱漫骂的,纠缠在一起的
examples: They were right: The snow-covered 18 holes of bunkers and straights made for a deliciously snarly winter track.
Green Day, the band that took snarly punk rock all the way to Broadway, will play one of this year's most anticipated sets.
The title character (Michael Caine), an elderly pensioner and former soldier, lives in a filthy South London council estate controlled by a snarly-faced youth gang.
